title: Vision Transformers for Medical Image Classification
text: This study compares vision transformers with convolutional neural networks for radiology image classification. Transformer models improved global context modeling but required careful compression for efficient inference.

title: Efficient Transformer Inference in Clinical Imaging
text: Knowledge distillation and token pruning reduced transformer latency in medical imaging pipelines. The compressed transformer matched CNN accuracy while lowering inference cost on edge hardware.

title: CNN Baselines for Radiology Workloads
text: Convolutional neural networks remain strong baselines for radiology workloads. The paper reports high throughput but weaker long-range dependency modeling compared with transformer architectures.

title: Hybrid Retrieval Graphs for Multi-Hop Question Answering
text: A graph retrieval system links evidence passages through citation and semantic dependency edges. Confidence pruning reduces noisy multi-hop branches and improves answer faithfulness.

title: Contradiction-Aware Reranking for Retrieval Augmented Generation
text: Contradiction-aware reranking penalizes evidence chains that contain conflicting claims. It is especially useful for comparative and causal queries in retrieval augmented generation.

title: Adaptive Query Decomposition for Complex Search
text: Complex queries benefit from decomposition into sub-intents before retrieval. Adaptive routing selects sparse, dense, or graph retrieval according to ambiguity and predicted hop count.
